# LiveMarkdown

> A free, browser-based Markdown editor with real-time preview, LaTeX math, Mermaid diagrams, syntax highlighting, and export to HTML or PDF. No installation or account required.

## What is LiveMarkdown?

LiveMarkdown is a single-page web application that runs entirely in the browser. It provides a split-pane interface: a plain-text Markdown editor on one side and a rendered live preview on the other.

It is built for developers, technical writers, students, and anyone who writes in Markdown. It requires no backend, no account, and no installation — open the page and start writing.

## Core features

- Real-time Markdown rendering (120ms debounce) via marked.js (GFM)
- Syntax highlighting for 100+ programming languages via highlight.js
- LaTeX math equations — inline `$…$` and display `$$…$$` via KaTeX
- Mermaid diagrams — flowchart, sequence, pie, git, and more
- Find & replace with live highlight overlay in the editor
- Export to `.md`, self-contained `.html`, and PDF (browser print)
- Drag & drop `.md` / `.txt` file open
- Auto-save to localStorage (content restored on reload)
- 4 layout modes: editor left, right, top, bottom
- Synchronized scrolling between editor and preview
- Dark mode and light mode
- Preview font family (Serif / Sans / Mono) and size controls
- Spell check toggle
- Keyboard shortcuts panel

## Technology

- Vanilla JavaScript (ES6+), HTML5, CSS3 — no framework, no build step
- Marked.js (Markdown parsing)
- Highlight.js (syntax highlighting)
- KaTeX (math rendering)
- Mermaid.js (diagram rendering)

## Usage

Open https://livemarkdown.github.io/ in any modern browser. Type Markdown in the left pane, see the rendered output in the right pane instantly. No login required.

## License

MIT — free to use, fork, and contribute.

## Source

https://github.com/forhadkhan/livemarkdown

## Contact / Contributions

Pull requests welcome via the main repository. See README.md for contributing guidelines.
